title: Metastelma brasiliense
intro: Metastelma brasiliense, also known as Brazilian Metastelma, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to Brazil. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, providing relief from various health issues such as fever, wounds, and respiratory infections. Bioactive constituents of Metastelma brasiliense include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Metastelma brasiliense is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant is further classified under the subfamily Asclepiadoideae, which is a group of plants commonly known as the milkweed family. Metastelma brasiliense is a species of Metastelma, a genus of flowering plants native to the Americas. The classification of Metastelma brasiliense is also known as a member of the order Gentianales. This plant is native to South America and is often found in tropical regions.
uses: Metastelma brasiliense, a plant native to Brazil, has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits.
